posts.content: LLM Knowledge Bases. Something I'm finding very useful recently: using LLMs to build personal knowledge bases for various topics of research interest. In this way, a large fraction of my recent token throughput is going less into manipulating code, and more into manipulating...
posts.highlights: Karpathy is shifting focus from coding to using LLMs to build and manage personal knowledge bases for research, indicating a move towards knowledge compounding and organization.
posts.worthReading: It reveals a practical, high-level workflow shift for an AI expert, moving from pure code generation to structured knowledge management using LLMs.

posts.content: LLMs are emerging as a new kind of intelligence, simultaneously a lot smarter than I expected and a lot dumber than I expected.
posts.highlights: Karpathy expresses the dual-nature surprise of LLM capabilities, acknowledging both their advanced and surprisingly limited aspects.
posts.worthReading: It captures a nuanced, expert perspective on the current state and paradoxical nature of LLM intelligence.

posts.content: Three days ago I left autoresearch tuning nanochat for ~2 days on depth=12 model.

posts.worthReading: It shows direct, technical experimentation with automated fine-tuning workflows on specific model architectures.

posts.content: Revisiting Sutton's Bitter Lesson in the wake of GPT-5.
